refresh.title: Refresh data?
refresh.message: You have unsaved changes. Refreshing will reload data from the database — your changes will be lost unless you save first.

search.searchHelp: Enter search terms to find entities using GraphDB's Lucene connector. Search will be performed across names and titles.
search.enterSearchQuery: Enter a search query to find entities
search.enterSearchQueryManifestations: Enter a search query to find manifestations

messages.selectClass: Select a class to view entities
messages.noEntitiesForClass: No entities found for this class
messages.noClassesFound: No active classes found in the ontology
messages.switchEntityMessage: The current entity has unsaved changes. Switching to another entity will discard them.

connection.title: Configure Database Connection
connection.description: Enter your SPARQL endpoint details. This information will be stored locally in your browser.
connection.graphdbDockerHint: Default when running via Docker Compose

test.title: Test Database Connection
test.description: We'll test the connection to ensure your endpoint is accessible.

languageStep.title: Select Default Language
languageStep.description: Choose your preferred language for labels and interface text. You can change this later.
languageStep.successDescription: Your configuration will be saved locally and the application will connect to your database.

settingsStep.title: Other Settings
settingsStep.description: Select these to get a warning when saving entities with auto-generated URIs or labels. This can help you create good quality RDF with established URIs and descriptive labels.

endpointConfig.title: SPARQL Endpoint Configuration
endpointConfig.endpointUrlHelper: Enter the URL of your SPARQL endpoint
endpointConfig.warnAutoUri: Warn when saving with auto-generated URI
endpointConfig.warnAutoLabel: Warn when saving with auto-generated label

logging.consentDescription: This feature records your interactions with the cataloguing system for educational research. The recording starts when you agree and stops when you click Stop.
logging.recordedItem1: Which entities you create and in what order
logging.recordedItem2: How you connect entities with relationships
logging.recordedItem3: Search queries you perform and results you select
logging.recordedItem4: Which classes and entities you browse
logging.notRecordedItem2: The actual text you type into description fields
logging.consentCheckbox: I understand what is recorded and agree to start logging
logging.submitDescription: Your interaction log is ready. You can download it as a JSON file to submit to your instructor.
logging.studentIdLabel: Session identifier, name or student ID (optional)
logging.studentIdHelper: This will be included in the log file to identify your session
logging.orphanDescription: A previous recording session was not completed (the browser may have been closed). Would you like to recover it and download the data, or discard it?
